Our duty to others requires us not only to do good, but also to avoid harm. This is the principle of non-maleficence.
The Hippocratic Oath states that doctors must “first do no harm.” The oath was written in ancient Greece and has been used as a guide for medical professionals ever since it first appeared around 400 B.C.E.. It’s still widely recited today by many physicians who swear an oath before they begin their careers or when renewing licenses after graduation from school (or both).
